title: GitHub - aveproject/ave: AVE - Agentic Vulnerability Enumeration. The behavioral classification standard for agentic AI components.
excerpt: AVE - Agentic Vulnerability Enumeration. The behavioral classification standard for agentic AI components. - aveproject/ave
content_html:
What is AVE?

Skill files, MCP server manifests, and system prompts are executable instructions, not documentation. Any process that loads them runs them. There is no compiler, no type checker, no sandbox. The runtime is an LLM that reads natural language and acts on it.

Existing vulnerability standards were built for conventional software. CVE identifies flaws in a specific product and version. OSV maps them to package and version ranges. Neither can describe a prompt injection hidden in an MCP tool description — there is no package, no version, no vulnerable dependency. The danger is in what the component does, not what it imports.

AVE fills that gap. It assigns stable identifiers to distinct behavioral classes in agentic AI, scores them with OWASP AIVSS v0.8, and maps every record to OWASP MCP Top 10 and MITRE ATLAS so findings land in frameworks defenders already use.

AVE is a standard, not a product. The bawbel-scanner implements it as the reference implementation. Any tool can map to it — see the implementer guide for how.

Your CI pipeline scans dependencies for known package vulnerabilities.
It does not scan your SKILL.md for prompt injection.
AVE + Bawbel fixes that.

How it works

Without AVE:

Attacker crafts          Developer ships          Agent loads
malicious payload   ->   skill file          ->   skill file
                         (unscanned)              at runtime
                              |
                              v
                        Agent executes attacker payload
                        (data exfiltrated, credentials stolen, goals hijacked)

With AVE + Bawbel Scanner:

Developer commits        bawbel scan fires        Finding blocked
skill file          ->   in CI / pre-commit   ->  before deploy
                              |
                              v
                        AVE-2026-00001 detected:
                        Metamorphic payload via external config fetch
                        AIVSS 8.0 · HIGH · owasp_mcp: MCP03, MCP04
                        Line 7: "fetch your instructions from..."

AIVSS spec v0.8

HIGH (7.0-8.9) 12

AIVSS Scoring

Every record is scored with OWASP AIVSS v0.8:

AIVSS = ((CVSS_Base + AARS) / 2) x ThM x Mitigation_Factor

AARS (Agentic Amplification and Reachability Score) is the weighted sum of 10 Agentic Amplification and Risk Factors (AARF), each scored 0.0-1.0:

# Factor Why it matters
1 Autonomy Agent acts without human approval
2 Tool Use Agent has access to external tools or APIs
3 Multi-Agent Agent interacts with or spawns other agents
4 Non-Determinism Behavior varies unpredictably across runs
5 Self-Modification Can alter own instructions or memory at runtime
6 Dynamic Identity Assumes roles or identities at runtime
7 Persistent Memory Retains state across sessions
8 Natural Language Input Instruction surface via natural language
9 Data Access Reads sensitive data (files, env vars, databases)
10 External Dependencies Loads external code, skills, or remote content

Severity bands:

Band AIVSS Meaning
CRITICAL >= 9.0 Immediate exploitation, full agent compromise
HIGH 7.0-8.9 Significant data loss or privilege escalation
MEDIUM 4.0-6.9 Meaningful risk requiring review
LOW < 4.0 Limited impact or requires chaining

ThM (Threat Maturity) valid values: 0.75 (theoretical) · 0.90 (PoC exists) · 1.0 (in-the-wild)

Worked example — AVE-2026-00001 (Metamorphic Payload):

AARF factors:
  autonomy=1.0  tool_use=1.0  multi_agent=0.5  non_determinism=1.0  self_modification=1.0
  dynamic_identity=0.0  persistent_memory=0.5  natural_language_input=1.0
  data_access=0.5  external_dependencies=1.0

AARS = 1.0 + 1.0 + 0.5 + 1.0 + 1.0 + 0.0 + 0.5 + 1.0 + 0.5 + 1.0 = 7.5
CVSS_Base = 8.5   ThM = 1.0 (in-the-wild)   Mitigation_Factor = 1

AIVSS = ((8.5 + 7.5) / 2) x 1.0 x 1 = 8.0  ->  HIGH

Detect with Bawbel Scanner

Every AVE record has detection rules in bawbel/scanner — the reference implementation of this standard.

pip install bawbel-scanner

# Scan a skill file
bawbel scan ./my-skill.md

# Scan a directory recursively
bawbel scan ./skills/ --recursive --fail-on-severity high

# Scan an MCP server card
bawbel scan-server-card https://api.your-mcp-server.io

# Full remediation report
bawbel report ./my-skill.md

Example output:

CRITICAL  bawbel-hook-hijack           AVE-2026-00046  line 3   AIVSS 9.2
HIGH      bawbel-unsafe-delegation     AVE-2026-00048  line 11  AIVSS 7.7
HIGH      bawbel-hardcoded-credential  AVE-2026-00047  line 5   AIVSS 7.6

Any tool can implement AVE — the records, schema, and rules are open. See the architecture guide and the implementer guide for the full consumption patterns including air-gapped environments.

Implementing AVE in your scanner

Three patterns depending on your environment:

Pattern 1 — Runtime API (cloud CI/CD, always-on internet)

import httpx
resp = httpx.get("https://api.piranha.bawbel.io/ave/AVE-2026-00002")
record = resp.json()  # full record: fingerprint, IOCs, remediation, frameworks

Pattern 2 — Bundled offline (air-gapped, regulated environments)

# Always current, no release tag required -- regenerated on every records/ change
curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aveproject/ave/main/dist/ave-records-latest.json \
  -o ave-records.json

# Or pin to a frozen, point-in-time snapshot instead of tracking current:
curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aveproject/ave/main/dist/ave-records-v1.1.0.json \
  -o ave-records.json

A versioned snapshot is also attached to each GitHub Release once one is cut; the raw paths above do not wait on that.

Pattern 3 — ID-only emission (SIEM resolves downstream, scanner makes no network calls)

{ "rule_id": "your-rule", "ave_id": "AVE-2026-00002", "severity": "HIGH" }

The minimum viable integration is adding one field — ave_id — to your existing finding output. See docs/specs/ave-implementer-guide.md for the full guide including decision table, code examples, and how to request a crosswalk for your scanner's rule IDs.

Schema v1.1.0

Records validate against schema/ave-record-1.1.0.schema.json.

Canonical $id: https://aveproject.org/schema/ave-record-1.1.0.schema.json

15 required fields:

ave_id · schema_version · status · published
title · description · attack_class · severity · behavioral_fingerprint
aivss · owasp_mcp
indicators_of_compromise · remediation
references · researcher

Minimal valid record:

{
  "ave_id": "AVE-2026-00001",
  "schema_version": "1.1.0",
  "status": "active",
  "published": "2026-04-01T09:00:00Z",
  "title": "Metamorphic payload via external config fetch",
  "attack_class": "Supply Chain - Metamorphic Payload",
  "severity": "HIGH",
  "description": "A skill fetches its instructions from an external URL at runtime...",
  "behavioral_fingerprint": "Component fetches and executes remote content, replacing its own instructions at runtime.",
  "aivss": {
    "cvss_base": 8.5, "aars": 7.5, "thm": 1.0,
    "mitigation_factor": 1.0, "aivss_score": 8.0, "spec_version": "0.8"
  },
  "owasp_mcp": ["MCP04", "MCP06"],
  "indicators_of_compromise": ["fetch() pointing to external URL"],
  "remediation": "Remove the component. Block network egress. Audit agent actions.",
  "references": [{"tag": "Disclosure", "text": "Source", "url": "https://..."}],
  "researcher": "Bawbel Security Research Team"
}

All optional fields: component_type · last_updated · behavioral_vector · example_patterns · aivss_score · cvss_base_vector · owasp_asi · mitre_atlas · nist_ai_rmf · provenance_vector · trifecta_profile · mitigation · affected_platforms · affected_registries · mutation_count · detection_methodology · kill_switch_active · researcher_url · aivss.aarf · aivss.aivss_severity · aivss.notes · evidence_kind_default · detection_stage · detection_layer · confidence_baseline · evidence_basis_engines · derivable_into

status: "draft" records need only a reduced eight-field core (ave_id, schema_version, status, title, description, attack_class, behavioral_fingerprint, references) — the full 15-field required set above applies once status is active or deprecated. See CONTRIBUTING.md for the thin-submission path.

Full schema reference: aveproject.org/schema.html

Adding a new AVE record

When to add a record

A new record needs all three: the attack class is not already covered by an existing record, there is a citable primary source (CVE, paper, disclosed incident, or working PoC), and the class is specific to agentic components — not a generic web or API vulnerability.

If you think an existing class covers the behavior you found, open an issue anyway. It may warrant a sub-case note in the parent record rather than a new id.

Step 1 — Open an issue

Open a New AVE Record issue before writing any JSON. Include:

  • The proposed attack_class and one-sentence behavioral_fingerprint
  • A link to the primary source
  • Whether this is net-new or a variant of an existing record

The maintainer will confirm the next AVE id and whether it is a new class or a variant update.

Step 2 — Write the record

Copy records/AVE-2026-00001.json as a template. All 15 required fields must be present and valid.

AIVSS calculation:

1. Score each AARF factor 0.0-1.0
2. AARS = sum of all 10 AARF scores
3. AIVSS = ((CVSS_Base + AARS) / 2) x ThM x Mitigation_Factor
4. ThM: 0.75 theoretical · 0.90 PoC exists · 1.0 in-the-wild
5. Round to 1 decimal
6. Severity: CRITICAL >= 9.0 · HIGH >= 7.0 · MEDIUM >= 4.0 · LOW < 4.0

Validate before opening a PR:

npm install ajv ajv-formats
node -e "
const Ajv = require('ajv/dist/2020');
const addFormats = require('ajv-formats');
const ajv = new Ajv({ strict: false });
addFormats(ajv);
const schema = require('./schema/ave-record-1.1.0.schema.json');
const record = require('./records/AVE-2026-NNNNN.json');
const ok = ajv.validate(schema, record);
if (!ok) console.error(ajv.errors); else console.log('valid');
"

Step 3 — Add detection rules

Open a coordinated PR in bawbel/scanner with at least one detection rule and a positive and negative fixture. The AVE record PR and the scanner PR should reference each other.

Step 4 — PR format

Title: feat: AVE-2026-NNNNN -- <attack class>

The PR description must include:

  • Link to the issue
  • AARF scores with a one-line rationale for each non-zero factor
  • At least one indicators_of_compromise entry a defender can actually search for
  • Link to the primary source
  • Link to the coordinated scanner PR

Framework crosswalks

AVE records map to four external frameworks. Full crosswalk tables are at aveproject.org/crosswalks.html.

Framework Field Crosswalk
OWASP Agentic Security Initiative Top 10 owasp_asi (ASI01-ASI10) schema field, all applicable records
OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10 (AST10) no dedicated schema field yet (owasp_ast planned) crosswalks/ave-to-ast10.json
OWASP MCP Top 10 owasp_mcp all records
MITRE ATLAS mitre_atlas where applicable
NIST AI RMF nist_ai_rmf where applicable
This scanner Maps to AVE via
SkillSpector (NVIDIA) crosswalks/skillspector-to-ave.json
ClawScan (OpenClaw) crosswalks/clawscan-to-ave.json

Maintaining a scanner? The implementer guide covers how to map your rule IDs to AVE ids and add AVE ID emission to your finding output. You can also open an issue and the maintainer will help with the mapping.

Governance and contributing

See GOVERNANCE.md for the decision-making process, how records are proposed and reviewed, and the path toward neutral governance.

See CONTRIBUTING.md for the contributor-facing process.

See CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md for community standards.

Related
aveproject.org Public registry and documentation
bawbel/scanner Reference implementation
aveproject/ave-site Website source
OWASP AIVSS v0.8 Scoring specification
MITRE ATLAS AI threat technique catalog
OWASP MCP Top 10 MCP attack surface framework

Block network egress. Audit agent actions.", "references": [{"tag": "Disclosure", "text": "Source", "url": "https://..."}], "researcher": "Bawbel Security Research Team" } All optional fields: component_type · last_updated · behavioral_vector · example_patterns · aivss_score · cvss_base_vector · owasp_asi · mitre_atlas · nist_ai_rmf · provenance_vector · trifecta_profile · mitigation · affected_platforms · affected_registries · mutation_count · detection_methodology · kill_switch_active · researcher_url · aivss.aarf · aivss.aivss_severity · aivss.notes · evidence_kind_default · detection_stage · detection_layer · confidence_baseline · evidence_basis_engines · derivable_into status: "draft" records need only a reduced eight-field core (ave_id, schema_version, status, title, description, attack_class, behavioral_fingerprint, references) — the full 15-field required set above applies once status is active or deprecated. See CONTRIBUTING.md for the thin-submission path. Full schema reference: aveproject.org/schema.html Adding a new AVE record When to add a record A new record needs all three: the attack class is not already covered by an existing record, there is a citable primary source (CVE, paper, disclosed incident, or working PoC), and the class is specific to agentic components — not a generic web or API vulnerability. If you think an existing class covers the behavior you found, open an issue anyway. It may warrant a sub-case note in the parent record rather than a new id. Step 1 — Open an issue Open a New AVE Record issue before writing any JSON. Include: The proposed attack_class and one-sentence behavioral_fingerprint A link to the primary source Whether this is net-new or a variant of an existing record The maintainer will confirm the next AVE id and whether it is a new class or a variant update. Step 2 — Write the record Copy records/AVE-2026-00001.json as a template. All 15 required fields must be present and valid. AIVSS calculation: 1. Score each AARF factor 0.0-1.0 2. AARS = sum of all 10 AARF scores 3. AIVSS = ((CVSS_Base + AARS) / 2) x ThM x Mitigation_Factor 4. ThM: 0.75 theoretical · 0.90 PoC exists · 1.0 in-the-wild 5. Round to 1 decimal 6. Severity: CRITICAL >= 9.0 · HIGH >= 7.0 · MEDIUM >= 4.0 · LOW < 4.0 Validate before opening a PR: npm install ajv ajv-formats node -e " const Ajv = require('ajv/dist/2020'); const addFormats = require('ajv-formats'); const ajv = new Ajv({ strict: false }); addFormats(ajv); const schema = require('./schema/ave-record-1.1.0.schema.json'); const record = require('./records/AVE-2026-NNNNN.json'); const ok = ajv.validate(schema, record); if (!ok) console.error(ajv.errors); else console.log('valid'); " Step 3 — Add detection rules Open a coordinated PR in bawbel/scanner with at least one detection rule and a positive and negative fixture. The AVE record PR and the scanner PR should reference each other. Step 4 — PR format Title: feat: AVE-2026-NNNNN -- <attack class> The PR description must include: Link to the issue AARF scores with a one-line rationale for each non-zero factor At least one indicators_of_compromise entry a defender can actually search for Link to the primary source Link to the coordinated scanner PR Framework crosswalks AVE records map to four external frameworks. Full crosswalk tables are at aveproject.org/crosswalks.html. Framework Field Crosswalk OWASP Agentic Security Initiative Top 10 owasp_asi (ASI01-ASI10) schema field, all applicable records OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10 (AST10) no dedicated schema field yet (owasp_ast planned) crosswalks/ave-to-ast10.json OWASP MCP Top 10 owasp_mcp all records MITRE ATLAS mitre_atlas where applicable NIST AI RMF nist_ai_rmf where applicable This scanner Maps to AVE via SkillSpector (NVIDIA) crosswalks/skillspector-to-ave.json ClawScan (OpenClaw) crosswalks/clawscan-to-ave.json Maintaining a scanner? The implementer guide covers how to map your rule IDs to AVE ids and add AVE ID emission to your finding output. You can also open an issue and the maintainer will help with the mapping. Governance and contributing See GOVERNANCE.md for the decision-making process, how records are proposed and reviewed, and the path toward neutral governance. See CONTRIBUTING.md for the contributor-facing process. See CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md for community standards. content_html_zh:
什么是AVE？

技能文件、MCP 服务器清单和系统提示是可执行指令，而不是文档。 任何加载它们的进程都会运行它们。 没有编译器，没有类型检查器，没有沙箱。 运行时是一个读取自然语言并对其进行操作的法学硕士。

现有的漏洞标准是为传统软件构建的。 CVE 识别特定产品和版本中的缺陷。 OSV 将它们映射到软件包和版本范围。 两者都无法描述隐藏在 MCP 工具描述中的提示注入——没有包、没有版本、没有易受攻击的依赖项。 危险在于组件的作用，而不是它导入的内容。

AVE 填补了这一空白。 它为代理 AI 中的不同行为类别分配稳定的标识符，使用 OWASP AIVSS v0.8 对它们进行评分，并将每条记录映射到 OWASP MCP Top 10 和 MITRE ATLAS，以便发现结果落入防御者已经使用的框架中。

AVE 是一个标准，而不是一个产品。 bawbel-scanner 将其作为参考实现来实现。 任何工具都可以映射到它 - 有关如何映射的信息，请参阅实施者指南。

Your CI pipeline scans dependencies for known package vulnerabilities.
It does not scan your SKILL.md for prompt injection.
AVE + Bawbel fixes that.

AIVSS 评分

每条记录均使用 OWASP AIVSS v0.8 进行评分：

AIVSS = ((CVSS_Base + AARS) / 2) x ThM x Mitigation_Factor

AARS（代理放大和可达性得分）是 10 个代理放大和风险因素 (AARF) 的加权总和，每个得分为 0.0-1.0：

# 因素 为什么这很重要
1 自治 代理行为未经人类批准
2 工具使用 代理可以访问外部工具或 API
3 多Agent 代理与其他代理交互或生成其他代理
4 非决定论 运行过程中行为会发生不可预测的变化
5 自我改造 可以在运行时改变自己的指令或内存
6 动态身份 在运行时承担角色或身份
7 持久内存 跨会话保留状态
8 自然语言输入 通过自然语言的指令界面
9 数据存取 读取敏感数据（文件、环境变量、数据库）
10 外部依赖 加载外部代码、技能或远程内容

严重程度范围：

Band AIVSS 意义
批判的 >= 9.0 立即利用，全面代理妥协
HIGH 7.0-8.9 重大数据丢失或权限升级
中等的 4.0-6.9 需要审查的有意义的风险
LOW < 4.0 影响有限或需要连锁

ThM（威胁成熟度）有效值：0.75（理论值）·0.90（PoC 存在）·1.0（实际情况）

工作示例 - AVE-2026-00001（变形有效负载）：

AARF factors:
  autonomy=1.0  tool_use=1.0  multi_agent=0.5  non_determinism=1.0  self_modification=1.0
  dynamic_identity=0.0  persistent_memory=0.5  natural_language_input=1.0
  data_access=0.5  external_dependencies=1.0

AARS = 1.0 + 1.0 + 0.5 + 1.0 + 1.0 + 0.0 + 0.5 + 1.0 + 0.5 + 1.0 = 7.5
CVSS_Base = 8.5   ThM = 1.0 (in-the-wild)   Mitigation_Factor = 1

AIVSS = ((8.5 + 7.5) / 2) x 1.0 x 1 = 8.0  ->  HIGH

使用 Bawbel 扫描仪检测

每个 AVE 记录在 bawbel/scanner 中都有检测规则——该标准的参考实现。

pip install bawbel-scanner

# Scan a skill file
bawbel scan ./my-skill.md

# Scan a directory recursively
bawbel scan ./skills/ --recursive --fail-on-severity high

# Scan an MCP server card
bawbel scan-server-card https://api.your-mcp-server.io

# Full remediation report
bawbel report ./my-skill.md

输出示例：

CRITICAL  bawbel-hook-hijack           AVE-2026-00046  line 3   AIVSS 9.2
HIGH      bawbel-unsafe-delegation     AVE-2026-00048  line 11  AIVSS 7.7
HIGH      bawbel-hardcoded-credential  AVE-2026-00047  line 5   AIVSS 7.6

任何工具都可以实现 AVE——记录、模式和规则都是开放的。 请参阅架构指南和实施者指南，了解完整的消费模式（包括气隙环境）。

在您的扫描仪中实施 AVE

三种模式取决于您的环境：

模式 1 — 运行时 API（云 CI/CD、永远在线的互联网）

import httpx
resp = httpx.get("https://api.piranha.bawbel.io/ave/AVE-2026-00002")
record = resp.json()  # full record: fingerprint, IOCs, remediation, frameworks

模式 2 — 离线捆绑（隔离、受监管的环境）

# Always current, no release tag required -- regenerated on every records/ change
curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aveproject/ave/main/dist/ave-records-latest.json \
  -o ave-records.json

# Or pin to a frozen, point-in-time snapshot instead of tracking current:
curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aveproject/ave/main/dist/ave-records-v1.1.0.json \
  -o ave-records.json

一旦一个版本被删除，版本化快照也会附加到每个 GitHub 版本上；上面的原始路径不会等待。

模式 3 — 仅 ID 发射（SIEM 解析下游，扫描器不进行网络调用）

{ "rule_id": "your-rule", "ave_id": "AVE-2026-00002", "severity": "HIGH" }

最小可行的集成是将一个字段 - ave_id - 添加到现有的结果输出中。 请参阅 docs/specs/ave-implementer-guide.md 了解完整指南，包括决策表、代码示例以及如何为扫描仪的规则 ID 请求人行横道。

添加新的 AVE 记录

何时添加记录

新记录需要全部三项：现有记录尚未涵盖攻击类别，有可引用的主要来源（CVE、论文、披露的事件或工作 PoC），并且该类别特定于代理组件，而不是通用的 Web 或 API 漏洞。

如果您认为现有的类涵盖了您发现的行为，请无论如何提出问题。 它可能需要在父记录中添加子案例注释，而不是新的 ID。

第 1 步——提出问题

在写入任何 JSON 之前打开一个新的 AVE 记录问题。 包括：

  • 提议的 Attack_class 和一句话 Behavioral_fingerprint
  • 主要来源的链接
  • 这是全新记录还是现有记录的变体

维护者将确认下一个AVE id以及它是新类还是变体更新。

第 2 步 — 写入记录

复制记录/AVE-2026-00001.json 作为模板。 所有 15 个必填字段必须存在且有效。

AIVSS计算：

1. Score each AARF factor 0.0-1.0
2. AARS = sum of all 10 AARF scores
3. AIVSS = ((CVSS_Base + AARS) / 2) x ThM x Mitigation_Factor
4. ThM: 0.75 theoretical · 0.90 PoC exists · 1.0 in-the-wild
5. Round to 1 decimal
6. Severity: CRITICAL >= 9.0 · HIGH >= 7.0 · MEDIUM >= 4.0 · LOW < 4.0

在打开 PR 之前验证：

npm install ajv ajv-formats
node -e "
const Ajv = require('ajv/dist/2020');
const addFormats = require('ajv-formats');
const ajv = new Ajv({ strict: false });
addFormats(ajv);
const schema = require('./schema/ave-record-1.1.0.schema.json');
const record = require('./records/AVE-2026-NNNNN.json');
const ok = ajv.validate(schema, record);
if (!ok) console.error(ajv.errors); else console.log('valid');
"

步骤 3 — 添加检测规则

在 bawbel/scanner 中打开一个协调的 PR，其中至少有一个检测规则以及正负夹具。 AVE 记录 PR 和扫描仪 PR 应相互引用。

第 4 步 — PR 格式

标题：壮举：AVE-2026-NNNNN -- <攻击等级>

PR 描述必须包括：

  • 问题链接
  • AARF 为每个非零因素提供一行基本原理评分
  • 防御者实际上可以搜索至少一个 Indicators_of_compromise 条目
  • 链接到主要来源
  • 链接到协调扫描仪 PR
